attempts.trialNote: Headless-only candidate gave reward-station falling threat priority over close-body at W1205. It improved max progression but caused gameplay loss/recovery, so it remains rejected and is not live strategy.
Headless-only candidate kept falling-threat priority but interrupted with close-body escape at W1205. It still died from body contact at frame 2677, so it remains rejected.

Headless-only candidate released A before the contact jump window, then pressed A with left-up fire. Runtime still showed no safe jump and died from body contact at frame 2677, so W1205 needs earlier standing/clearance rather than a late contact jump.

Headless-only candidate stopped at W1188 with up-fire to clear the pre-contact soldier. Runtime showed the soldier continued into body contact and killed the player at frame 2691, so standing clear is rejected.
Headless-only candidate forced right-up fire through W1205. It still died from body contact at frame 2677 and then recovered into a long stall, so right-up through is rejected.
Headless-only candidate held right/down/fire in the contact window. Runtime still collided with the soldier at frame 2688, so waterline duck-under is rejected.

Headless-only candidate released B, then pulsed straight right-fire. It generated new straight bullets but they passed under/after the soldier and the player still died at frame 2677, so pulsed straight fire is rejected.

Headless-only candidate forced right/down/fire after W1141 low-lane retreat. It avoided death but reduced max progression to W1195 and remained in a long low-lane stall, so the recovery-only approach is rejected.

Headless-only candidate held vertical up-fire at W1196-W1212 to shoot the fixed target without left retreat. It died from upper body contact at frame 4581 before reducing fixed-target HP, so vertical station is rejected.

Headless-only candidate resumed right-up fire after the W1205 upper soldier passed. It broke the W1205 stall and reached W1555, but died at frame 2802 from same-lane body contact at W1151, so the recovery direction is useful but this unsafe version is rejected.

Headless-only candidate let same-lane body contact override W1205 recovery and resumed right-up fixed-target fire only after the immediate contact window. It broke the W1205 stall without an immediate death, but later died at W1360 from a new close upper-body contact, so it remains rejected.

Headless-only candidate inherited W1205 safe recovery and forced right-up fire through the W1360 upper-body station crowd. It reached W1758 in the danger segment, but died at W1726 from low/side body contact during down-left fire, so it remains rejected.

Headless-only candidate inherited W1205 safe recovery and W1360 station-crowd escape, then forced right/down fire at W1726 low-side contact. It pushed the run to W2001, but later died at W1660 from rear/side body contact while retreating up-left, so it remains rejected.

Headless-only candidate inherited W1205 safe recovery, W1360 station-crowd escape, and W1726 low-side body escape, then added a W1641-W1650 left-edge overhead body guard plus W1658-W1672 retreat-regression guard. It delayed the W1641 left-edge death to W1644 and recovered after the loss, but still died at frame 9867, so it remains rejected.

Headless-only candidate inherited W1205 safe recovery, W1360 station-crowd escape, W1726 low-side body escape, and W1660 retreat guard, then changed the W1641-W1650 left-edge overhead-body response from prone fire to right-jump up-fire. Runtime showed the jump was too late: pre-loss input was right+up+A+B at W1649, but the player still died at W1648 from slot13 body overlap, so it remains rejected.

Headless-only candidate inherited the prior W1205/W1360/W1726/W1660/W1641 chain and added an earlier W1648-W1650 left-edge precompression advance. It prevented the immediate W1648 body death and pushed active play to frame 11000, but still died later around W1678/W1679 from a close upper/body threat, so it remains rejected.

Headless-only candidate inherited W1648 precompression escape and tried to handle the W1678 forward-body window with right+down+B. It avoided death but proved that down+right is not usable as movement in this context: the run stalled at W1692 with 2959 stalled frames, so it remains rejected as a no-death stuck-loop example.

Headless-only candidate inherited W1648 precompression escape and replaced W1678 crouch carry with level fire advance. Runtime regressed: the player died at frame 9260 around W1692/W1693 from a same-lane contact threat, so it remains rejected.

Headless-only candidate inherited W1648 precompression escape and tried to jump-clear the W1685-W1700 low-stack contact window with right+up+A+B. Runtime regressed: the player still died at frame 9254 around W1700/W1701 from same-lane/low-stack body contact, so late stack jump is rejected. Next route work must start earlier than the W1692 stack.

Headless-only candidate inherited W1648 precompression and tried to create a jump edge for the W1678 upper-body threat by releasing A before right+up+A+B. The first narrow version reached maxProgression 1942 but died earlier at W1682; the extended version regressed to maxProgression 1812 and died at frame 9264 around W1687 from same-lane body contact. The useful lesson is that W1686 is already too late; the route needs earlier pre-clear or approach correction before the same-lane stack forms.

Headless-only candidate inherited the W1648 precompression route and tried to pre-clear the W1664 same-lane body by using a down-right fire pulse to release and re-press B before the low contact stack formed. Runtime generated player bullets, but it regressed to an earlier frame-9862 death around W1658/W1659 from an overhead-left body threat, so this pulse does not solve the route. The useful lesson is that W1664 pre-clear must not weaken the W1658 overhead-body guard; the next candidate should address the overhead threat first or alter the approach before W1658.

Headless-only candidate inherited W1648 precompression, guarded the W1658 overhead-left body with down-right fire, then allowed W1664 same-lane preclear. Runtime still produced a gameplay loss at frame 10193 around W1726, recovered to the bridge segment, and accumulated 1272 stall frames. It is rejected. The useful lesson is that W1658 guard can move the blocker later than W1664 preclear alone, but combining it with the current preclear/route still loses control before W1726.

Headless-only candidate inherited W1648 precompression, W1658 overhead guard, and W1664 same-lane preclear, then added a W1726 grounded overhead duck-advance hold. It converted the W1726 pre-loss input into down-right fire, but the full runtime regressed to a W1683/W1684 close-body death with up-left jump, max W1784 and final W82. It is rejected. The useful lesson is that a local W1726 hold is not enough; the route must avoid entering the W1683 low/upper body stack before the fixed-threat platform.

Headless-only candidate added a W1436-W1444 airborne descent lower-body right-carry response after the formal survival baseline died with down-left fire at W1439/W1440. Runtime changed the pre-loss input to down-right fire, but death still occurred on frame 9414 with the lower body contact at W1443. It is rejected. The useful lesson is that this blocker is not solved by swapping left retreat to right carry after descent; the route must prevent the lower-body contact earlier, likely by changing the jump/landing timing before W1436.

Headless-only candidate added a W1448-W1456 airborne fixed-contact right/down fire response, then inherited the W1440 descent lower-body right-carry patch. Runtime still died at frame 9414/W1445 with slot5 lower-body contact dx=-1 dy=20. It is rejected. Lesson: entering W1454 with the lower soldier already below-forward is too late; right/down carry does not prevent the body collision.

Headless-only candidate tested a W1454 right/down fire pulse that releases B for two frames before firing again, based on bullet evidence showing old shots drifting left of slot5. Runtime still died at frame 9414/W1445 with the same lower-body contact. It is rejected. Lesson: this blocker is not solved by B pulsing or late contact shooting; next work must change the earlier jump/landing route before W1436-W1454.

Headless-only candidate moved the route-formation fix earlier to W1444-W1464 by suppressing airborne left pull around the fixed-target and slot5 convergence. Runtime eliminated the previous W9414/W1445 first death and moved the first loss to frame 11978/W1735 in the danger-survive segment. It is still rejected because the run dies, but the useful lesson is validated: route formation must preserve rightward air control before the lower-body contact forms. Next target is the W1735 danger-survive body-contact window.

Headless-only candidate inherited W1456 route formation and W1726 low-side body right carry. Runtime still died at frame 11978 around W1739/W1740 with slot12 same-lane contact. It moved the final post-death position slightly right but did not prevent the collision, so it is rejected. Lesson: W1735 needs an earlier same-lane contact prevention, not only low-stack inheritance.

Headless-only candidate added a narrow W1736-W1752 same-lane contact right-carry override for the W1735 failure. Runtime changed the final active input to down+right+B and moved the collision to W1743/X120, but still died on the same active frame. It is rejected. Lesson: the contact window is already formed by W11977; next work must move earlier than the last active frame and prevent slot12 from reaching the body lane.
